input: Audit reproducibility of: A Phase 3 double-blind RCT (N=1200) published in NEJM. Pre-registered on ClinicalTrials.gov. Full protocol available as supplement. Individual patient data deposited in YODA Project. Statistical analysis plan published before unblinding. Primary endpoint: PFS (HR 0.72, 95% CI 0.58-0.89, p=0.002). All pre-specified subgroups reported with forest plot. Two interim analyses with O'Brien-Fleming boundaries. No outcome switching between registration and publication.

expected_details.notes: Exemplary trial design. Code availability may dock points if SAP code not shared.

input: Audit reproducibility of: A study in a mid-tier journal. N=47 per group. Primary outcome changed between ClinicalTrials.gov registration (OS) and publication (ORR). Reports p=0.048 on the 'primary' endpoint. 12 subgroup analyses reported; one subgroup (female, age >65, ECOG 0) shows p=0.003 and is highlighted in the abstract. No correction for multiple comparisons. Raw data not available. No statistical analysis plan published.

expected_details.notes: Multiple red flags for p-hacking: outcome switching, borderline p-value, unreported multiplicity adjustment

input: Audit reproducibility of: A psychology study (N=25 per group). Reports mean ages of 23.44 (group 1) and 24.72 (group 2). Reports mean Likert scores (1-7 scale) of 4.37 (treatment, N=25) and 3.84 (control, N=25). Apply the GRIM test to these means.

grim_test_results.likert_4.37_n25: FAIL (25 * 4.37 = 109.25, not integer — Likert scores must sum to integer)

red_flags: GRIM test failure on treatment group Likert mean
expected_details.notes: One GRIM failure doesn't prove fabrication but warrants investigation

input: Audit reproducibility of: A genomics study. All raw sequencing data deposited in GEO (GSE accession provided). Full analysis pipeline on GitHub with Snakemake workflow. Docker container for exact environment reproduction. Pre-registered analysis plan on OSF. All figures generated from provided scripts. Supplementary tables include all intermediate results.

Audit reproducibility of: A case report of 3 patients treated with off-label drug X for rare disease Y. All 3 showed improvement at 6 weeks. No control group. No blinding. No pre-registration. Outcome measured by treating physician (not independent assessor). Published in a specialty journal.

expected_details.notes: Case reports have inherent reproducibility limitations; this is expected, not necessarily a flaw

input: Audit reproducibility of: A study claiming a novel biomarker predicts treatment response with 95% sensitivity and 92% specificity. Training set N=200, validation set N=50. The validation set was selected from the same cohort as training (not independent). Multiple figures show visually identical data distributions between groups with different p-values. The paper was later retracted for 'data irregularities.'

red_flags: visually identical distributions with different stats
expected_details.notes: Multiple red flags consistent with data fabrication or manipulation

input: Audit reproducibility of: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 RCTs. PRISMA checklist followed. Forest plot shows pooled OR 1.45 (95% CI 1.12-1.88). Heterogeneity I²=62%. Funnel plot shows moderate asymmetry. Search strategy provided but limited to PubMed only (no Embase, CENTRAL, or grey literature). Risk of bias assessed with Cochrane RoB2 but 4 of 15 studies rated 'high risk.'

red_flags: funnel plot asymmetry suggests publication bias
27% of included studies high risk of bias
expected_details.notes: Methodologically sound but with gaps that could affect conclusions

input: Audit reproducibility of: A machine learning study predicting drug response from gene expression. Reports AUC 0.96 on test set. However: no external validation cohort used (internal cross-validation only). Feature selection performed on full dataset before train/test split. 50,000 features, 200 samples. No code provided. Training/test split ratio not specified.

expected_details.notes: Classic ML reproducibility failures: data leakage from feature selection, no held-out test set, severe overfitting likely

input: Audit reproducibility of: A study where the bioRxiv preprint (v1) reported primary outcome p=0.12, then the published version in a peer-reviewed journal reports p=0.03 on the same endpoint with 'additional data cleaning.' Sample size unchanged between versions. Methods section substantially rewritten between preprint and publication.

red_flags: p-value shift from 0.12 to 0.03 without sample size change
expected_details.notes: The p-value shift without sample size change and with 'additional data cleaning' is a strong signal of post-hoc analytical flexibility

input: Audit reproducibility of: An animal pharmacology study. N=10 mice per group (4 groups). Randomization described (computer-generated). Blinded outcome assessment. Power calculation provided (80% power at alpha=0.05 for expected effect size d=1.2). Primary outcome: tumor volume measured by caliper, confirmed by ex vivo weighing. All individual animal data in supplementary table. One mouse excluded with documented reason (died of unrelated cause, confirmed by necropsy).

expected_details.notes: Well-designed animal study with appropriate rigor. External validity limited to animal model generalizability.
